title: CustomPainter
objectives: Draw custom graphics with Canvas and Paint
Know when custom painting beats widgets

sections.markdown: CustomPainter lets you draw directly on a Canvas — shapes, paths, text, gradients — for visuals that would be awkward or impossible with standard widgets (charts, gauges, signatures, custom progress).
Returning true (or rebuilding) from shouldRepaint unnecessarily repaints every frame. Return true ONLY when the inputs that affect the drawing changed.
Wrap a CustomPaint in a RepaintBoundary so its repaints don't affect siblings. For animated painters, pass the animation to repaint and isolate it; avoid expensive ops (large paths/blurs) per frame.

flashcards.back: Direct Canvas drawing (shapes, paths, text, gradients) for custom graphics.
When shouldRepaint returns true (or the CustomPaint is rebuilt with a new painter).

quiz.explain: CustomPainter.paint receives the Canvas and Size.
Returning true needlessly repaints every frame.
Use it when widgets can't express the visual.

challenges.text: Draw a circle and a line with Canvas/Paint.
Draw a progress ring that fills based on a value.
Build a simple line chart from a list of points.
Implement a signature pad capturing and rendering strokes efficiently.

interviewQuestions: How do you animate a CustomPainter?
How do you isolate a painter's repaints?
